method_atom: An iterative coarse-graining/renormalization contraction scheme (corner-transfer-matrix renormalization group) for computing the collective statistical behavior of many two-state units coupled by pairwise nearest-neighbor interactions that favour agreement, arranged on a discrete network whose underlying geometry has constant negative curvature. From the contracted partition function one locates a symmetry-breaking ordering transition (critical value of the control parameter) and its scaling exponents, and tracks how these quantities converge toward a mean-field / infinitely-branching tree limit as the local connectivity is increased.
object_atom: The two-dimensional ferromagnetic Ising model of magnetic spins placed on regular tessellations of the hyperbolic plane by p-sided polygons (p>=5: pentagons, hexagons, etc.) having constant negative scalar curvature, together with its p->infinity Bethe-lattice limit.
brief_reason: The negatively-curved lattice geometry, the RG transfer-matrix contraction technique, and the critical-point/scaling-exponent extraction toward a mean-field tree limit are the transferable machinery, while ferromagnetic Ising spins on specific hyperbolic polygon tilings are the physics-specific object.
